user.title: ⚡ Welcome to CloudEMS v1.5
user.description:
**CloudEMS** is your smart energy management system for Home Assistant.

✅ NILM device detection · Phase limiting · Solar curtailment · EPEX prices · Dynamic EV charging · Peak shaving

📖 [Documentation]({support_url}) · ☕ [Support CloudEMS]({buy_me_coffee_url}) · 🌐 [cloudems.eu]({website})

![]({diagram_url})

data_description.energy_prices_country: Select your country to fetch the correct EPEX day-ahead electricity prices.
data_description.wizard_mode: Basic: quick setup with essential sensors. Advanced: full control over all sensors, inverters and P1.
data_description.battery_sensor: ⚠️ Cloud batteries (e.g. Zonneplan) may have 30–300s delay. CloudEMS applies EMA smoothing automatically.
data_description.grid_sensor: Must be in Watts (W), not kWh or kW. P1 sensors typically report in W.
data_description.solar_sensor: Must be in Watts (W). Some inverters report in kW — use a template to multiply by 1000.

grid_connection.title: ⚡ Grid connection
grid_connection.description:
Select the fuse size that matches your installation. This sets the maximum current per phase that CloudEMS will enforce.

![]({diagram_url})

data_description.phase_preset: E.g. 3×25A = 3-phase with 25 A per phase. Select 'Custom' to enter values manually.

phase_custom.title: ⚡ Custom current limits
phase_custom.description: Enter the maximum current allowed per phase. CloudEMS will never exceed these values.

data_description.max_current_l1: Maximum grid current on phase L1 — typically your fuse rating (e.g. 25 A).
data_description.max_current_l2: Maximum grid current on phase L2. Only relevant for 3-phase installations.
data_description.max_current_l3: Maximum grid current on phase L3. Only relevant for 3-phase installations.

grid_sensors.title: 🔌 Grid sensors
grid_sensors.description:
CloudEMS detected **{detected}** sensor(s) automatically.

**Net sensor:** Use a single sensor reporting net grid power (positive = import).
**Separate import / export:** Use this if your integration reports them as two sensors.

The fallback voltage ({mains_voltage} V) is used to calculate power from current (P = V × I) when no direct power sensor is available.

![]({diagram_url})

data.use_separate_import_export: Use separate import and export sensors

data_description.use_separate_import_export: Enable when your meter reports import and export as two separate sensors instead of one net value.
data_description.grid_sensor: Main grid power sensor. Positive = importing, negative = exporting.
data_description.import_power_sensor: Power consumed from the grid (always positive).
data_description.export_power_sensor: Power fed back to the grid (always positive).
data_description.mains_voltage: Used as fallback for P = V × I when no voltage sensor is connected. Default: 230 V (EU).

phase_sensors.title: 🔌 Per-phase sensors (optional)
phase_sensors.description:
These sensors are **optional** — CloudEMS works without them.

Current sensors (A) enable per-phase limiting. Voltage sensors improve accuracy. If a power sensor is missing, CloudEMS will calculate power from current × voltage automatically.

Showing {phase_count} phase(s) based on your grid connection.

![]({diagram_url})

data_description.phase_sensors_L1: Current in amperes on phase L1. Used for per-phase limiting and balancing.
data_description.phase_sensors_L2: Current in amperes on phase L2.
data_description.phase_sensors_L3: Current in amperes on phase L3.
data_description.voltage_sensor_l1: Actual voltage on L1. Improves accuracy; used for P = V × I fallback.
data_description.power_sensor_l1: Direct power measurement on L1 (W). Use if available for maximum accuracy. Falls back to V × I automatically.

solar_ev.title: 🌞 Solar & EV
solar_ev.description:
Connect your solar inverter, battery and EV charger. All fields are optional.

![]({diagram_url})

data.solar_sensor: Solar inverter power sensor (W or kW)
data.battery_sensor: Battery / storage power sensor (W or kW)
data.enable_solar_dimmer: Curtail solar when EPEX price is negative

data_description.solar_sensor: Total PV power output in W or kW.
data_description.battery_sensor: Battery charge/discharge power. Positive = charging, negative = discharging.
data_description.ev_charger_entity: A number entity controlling the EV charger current setpoint (e.g. Easee, OCPP, go-e).
data_description.enable_solar_dimmer: Curtail or switch off your inverter during hours with negative EPEX prices to prevent paying to export.
data_description.negative_price_threshold: Price level below which solar curtailment activates. Default: 0.00 EUR/kWh.

inverter_count.title: ☀️ How many inverters?
inverter_count.description:
Select the number of PV inverters in your installation. Select **0** to skip inverter management.

CloudEMS will self-learn azimuth, tilt and peak power from your production history.

📖 [Multi-inverter docs]({docs_url})

inverter_detail.title: ☀️ Inverter {inverter_num} of {total}

data.inv_azimuth: Azimuth (°) — optional, leave blank to self-learn
data.inv_tilt: Tilt angle (°) — optional, leave blank to self-learn
data.inv_rated_power: Rated inverter power (W) — optional, for accurate clipping detection

features.title: 🚀 Features
features.description: Enable the features you need. You can change these at any time via Settings → Integrations → CloudEMS → Configure.

data.dynamic_loading: Dynamic loading — charge EV during cheapest EPEX hours
data.peak_shaving_enabled: Peak shaving — stay under monthly peak demand limit
data.battery_scheduler_enabled: Enable battery scheduling based on prices

data_description.dynamic_loading: Increase EV charging current during the cheapest EPEX hours and reduce it during expensive hours.
data_description.dynamic_load_price_threshold: Price at or below which an hour is considered 'cheap' for EV charging.
data_description.cost_tracking_enabled: Track daily, monthly and yearly energy costs using live EPEX prices.
data_description.phase_balance_enabled: Automatically redistribute loads to keep phase imbalance within the threshold.
data_description.phase_balance_threshold_a: Maximum allowed current difference between phases before rebalancing is triggered (A).
data_description.peak_shaving_enabled: Limit the monthly peak demand by switching off sheddable loads when demand exceeds the limit. Relevant for capacity-tariff countries (BE, FR).
data_description.price_alert_high_eur_kwh: Send an alert when the EPEX price exceeds this value. Default 0.30 €/kWh.
data_description.nilm_min_confidence: Devices with lower confidence are hidden in the dashboard. Default 0.65.

peak_config.title: 📊 Peak shaving settings
peak_config.description: Configure the peak demand limit and which loads CloudEMS may switch off to stay under it.

data.peak_shaving_assets: Sheddable loads (entities to switch off during peak)

data_description.peak_shaving_limit_w: Maximum allowed grid import power in Watt. When exceeded, CloudEMS will switch off sheddable loads.
data_description.peak_shaving_assets: Select switches, lights, or other controllable entities that may be turned off to reduce peak demand.

ai_config.title: 🤖 AI & NILM
ai_config.description:
CloudEMS can identify appliances from your power data (NILM — Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring).

**Priority:** Built-in pattern matching → Ollama (local) → Cloud AI

**None:** Pattern matching only, no external calls.
**Ollama:** Local LLM on your own hardware — privacy-first.
**CloudEMS / OpenAI / Anthropic:** Cloud AI for highest accuracy.

🔑 [Get a CloudEMS API key]({premium_url})

data.cloud_api_key: API key (required for CloudEMS, OpenAI and Anthropic)
data.nilm_min_confidence: Minimum confidence to accept a device detection (0.30–0.99)

data_description.ai_provider: Select which AI backend CloudEMS uses to identify appliances. Ollama and built-in are local; cloud options require an API key.
data_description.cloud_api_key: Enter your API key for the selected cloud provider. Leave blank when using None or Ollama.
data_description.nilm_min_confidence: Detections below this confidence are ignored. Lower = more results but more false positives. Default: 0.65.

ollama_config.title: 🤖 Ollama settings
ollama_config.description: Configure the Ollama server that CloudEMS will use for local AI inference.

data_description.ollama_host: Hostname or IP address of your Ollama instance.
data_description.ollama_port: TCP port Ollama listens on. Default is 11434.
data_description.ollama_model: Name of the Ollama model to use. Recommended: llama3, mistral or phi3 (smallest).

advanced.title: 📡 P1 smart meter
advanced.description: Optionally connect a P1/DSMR smart meter directly via TCP. Most users already have P1 data via the DSMR integration — only enable this if you want a direct TCP connection.

data_description.p1_host: IP address of your P1 gateway (e.g. HomeWizard P1, DSMR-reader).
data_description.p1_port: TCP port of the P1 gateway. Default is 8088.

battery_count.title: 🔋 Batteries — Count
battery_count.description:
How many battery systems do you want to configure?

Each battery is configured individually with a power sensor, SoC and capacity.

prices.title: 💶 Prices & Tax
prices.description: Configure energy tax and supplier markup for accurate cost calculations.

init.title: ⚙️ CloudEMS — Configure
init.description: Select the section you want to edit.

sensors.title: 🔌 Grid Sensors
sensors.description: Update grid sensor, phase count and current limits.

data.use_separate_import_export: Use separate import and export sensors

phase_sensors.title: ⚡ Phase Sensors
phase_sensors.description: Per-phase current, voltage and power sensors. If a power sensor is not filled, CloudEMS will calculate power from current × voltage.

data.gas_sensor: Gas meter sensor (m³) — optional, alternative to P1
data.gas_price_fixed: Fixed gas price (€/m³) — used when no sensor

ai_opts.title: 🤖 AI & NILM
ai_opts.description: Select the AI provider and adjust NILM detection settings. NILM-detected devices appear as sensors under Settings → Devices → CloudEMS. You can confirm, rename or dismiss them there.

data_description.nilm_min_confidence: Detections below this confidence score are ignored. 0.65 is a good default.

data.outside_temp_entity: Outdoor temperature sensor (for heat demand mode)

data_description.congestion_threshold_w: Import power above which congestion is flagged. Typically your capacity tariff limit.
data_description.battery_chemistry: Select your battery chemistry. LFP degrades slower than NMC.
data_description.outside_temp_entity: Optional: link an outdoor temperature sensor. CloudEMS will prioritise heat pump / CV when cold and price is low.

inverter_detail_opts.title: ☀️ Inverter {inverter_num} of {total}

data.inv_azimuth: Azimuth (°) — optional, leave empty to self-learn
data.inv_tilt: Tilt angle (°) — optional, leave empty to self-learn

batteries_opts.title: 🔋 Batteries
batteries_opts.description: How many batteries do you want to configure?

battery_detail_opts.title: 🔋 Battery {battery_num} of {total}
battery_detail_opts.description:
Configure battery **{battery_num}**.

Leave capacity and power at 0 — CloudEMS will self-learn them.

prices_opts.title: 💶 Prices & Tax
prices_opts.description:
Set whether energy prices are shown including tax and VAT.

The supplier markup is indicative — verify your own contract.

data.supplier_markup: Custom markup (€/kWh) — when 'Custom markup' is selected
